name: North Dakota Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to North Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: North Dakota Board of Dietetic Practice (NDBODP)

address.description:
North Dakota Board of Dietetic Practice
PO Box 7079
Bismarck, ND 58507-7079

requiredDocuments.name: Official Application
requiredDocuments.description: Application form accurately and completely filled out.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration (for LRDs).

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Required, especially if applying via the Licensed Nutritionist pathway.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a North Dakota dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial cost is a $75 application fee for both LRDs and LNs. A provisional license is $25.

mainEntity.name: When does the North Dakota license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: North Dakota has a 5-year renewal cycle that typically matches your CDR registration cycle, ensuring continuing education hours align. The renewal fee is $45.

mainEntity.name: Does North Dakota have a temporary license for non-residents?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, North Dakota offers a 'Limited Practice Without a License' option for $25. This is intended for dietitians practicing in the state for fewer than 30 days per year.

mainEntity.name: Does North Dakota have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in North Dakota. To work in North Dakota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to North Dakota?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in North Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
